title: Frequently Asked Questions
description: Common questions from Indians travelling to or living in Belgium.

items.question: Do Indian citizens need a visa to visit Belgium?
items.answer: Yes. Indian passport holders need a Schengen Type C visa for tourism or short business visits of up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Apply through VFS Global.

items.question: How long does a Belgium visa take to process?
items.answer: Typically around 15 calendar days for a complete application, but it can take up to 30-60 days during busy periods, so apply as early as possible (up to 6 months ahead, and no later than 15 days before travel).

items.question: What is the emergency number in Belgium?
items.answer: Dial 112 for any emergency (ambulance, fire or police) anywhere in Belgium — it's toll-free. You can also dial 101 directly for police or 100 for ambulance/fire.

items.question: Where is the Indian Embassy in Belgium located?
items.answer: The Embassy of India is at 217 Chaussée de Vleurgat, 1050 Brussels. It covers Belgium, Luxembourg and the EU institutions.

items.question: How do I get from Brussels Airport into the city?
items.answer: The Brussels Airport Express train takes about 18 minutes to Brussels-Central (~€8.90). The STIB Airport Bus (Line 12) is a cheaper alternative into the EU Quarter (~€7.90 with a MOBIB or contactless card).

items.question: What currency is used in Belgium?
items.answer: The Euro (EUR). Contactless card payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are common in all cities.

items.question: Is Belgium safe for solo travellers?
items.answer: Belgium is generally very safe. As in any European city, be alert for pickpocketing in crowded tourist areas and on public transport.

items.question: Can I find vegetarian and Jain food in Belgium?
items.answer: Yes, especially in Brussels and Antwerp (particularly around the Diamond District), which have dedicated Indian vegetarian and Jain-friendly restaurants.

items.question: What languages are spoken in Belgium?
items.answer: Belgium has three official languages: Dutch (Flanders), French (Wallonia) and German (small eastern region). Brussels is officially bilingual French/Dutch. English is widely understood in cities.

items.question: Do I need to register with the local municipality after arriving?
items.answer: Yes, if staying longer than 90 days, non-EU nationals must register their address at the local commune/gemeente (municipality) within 8 working days of arrival.

items.question: How do public transport tickets work across Belgium?
items.answer: Each region has its own operator — STIB (Brussels), De Lijn (Flanders), TEC (Wallonia) and SNCB (national trains). MOBIB cards and contactless bank cards work on most local networks; SNCB tickets are separate.

items.question: What should I pack for Belgium's weather?
items.answer: Belgium has a mild, rainy maritime climate. Pack layers, a waterproof jacket, and comfortable walking shoes year-round; winters are cold and grey, summers are mild.

items.question: Can I use my Indian driving licence in Belgium?
items.answer: An Indian driving licence with an International Driving Permit (IDP) can be used for short stays (up to 185 days). For residents, conversion to a Belgian licence is required within that window.

items.question: Are there Hindu temples or gurudwaras in Belgium?
items.answer: Yes — Brussels and Antwerp have Hindu temples and gurudwaras serving the Indian community. See the Indian Community section for details.

items.question: How do I renew my Indian passport while in Belgium?
items.answer: Passport renewal is handled by the Embassy of India in Brussels. Check indianembassybrussels.gov.in for the latest application forms and appointment system.

items.question: Is healthcare good in Belgium?
items.answer: Belgium has a high-quality healthcare system. Visitors should carry travel insurance; residents must enrol in mandatory health insurance (mutualité/ziekenfonds).

items.question: Can I work in Belgium on a tourist visa?
items.answer: No. A Schengen tourist visa does not permit employment. You need a work permit or single permit tied to a specific job offer.

items.question: What is the best way to travel between Belgian cities?
items.answer: SNCB trains are fast, frequent and connect all major cities — usually the best option over driving, especially between Brussels, Antwerp, Ghent and Bruges.

items.question: Do I need an adapter for electrical outlets in Belgium?
items.answer: Belgium uses the Type E plug (230V, 50Hz), the same as most of continental Europe. Indian plugs (Type C/D) will need an adapter.

items.question: How do I find English-speaking doctors in Belgium?
items.answer: Many doctors in Brussels and other major cities speak English, especially in international clinics. See the Healthcare section for guidance on finding one.

items.question: Are you looking to apply for a Single Permit on your own?
items.answer:
Yes. You can apply for a Single Permit on your own, provided you meet the eligibility requirements.

Information on Unlimited A Card (For Indian Nationals)

In both Brussels and Flanders, you may become eligible to apply for an Unlimited A Card (Unlimited Work Permit) after 4 years, subject to the applicable regional conditions.

A Single Permit consists of two parts:
- Work Permit – Approved by the regional authority, allowing you to work in Belgium.
- Residence Permit – Approved by the Federal Government based on the approved work permit, allowing you to legally reside in Belgium.

After completing the required period in the Brussels Region, you may apply for an Unlimited Work Permit (A Card – Unlimited).

Official Information
Brussels: https://economy-employment.brussels/single-permit-unlimited
Flanders: https://www.vlaanderen.be/en/work-permits-for-foreign-workers/work-permits-indefinite-term

Types of Permits

Permanent Residence: Allows you to live and work in Belgium without employment restrictions. You may continue to stay in Belgium even if you are not employed.

A Card (Limited): Work permit is linked to your employer. Residence remains valid while you continue to meet the employment conditions.

A Card (Unlimited): Work permit is not linked to a specific employer. You can change employers without applying for a new work permit. Your residence card generally remains valid for one year and must be renewed annually.

B / D / L Cards: Allow you to live and work in Belgium without restrictions.

Frequently Asked Question: My employment contract is permanent (undefined duration). How does the commune determine the validity of my residence card?
In Brussels, even if you have a permanent employment contract, the Unlimited A Card residence permit is generally issued with a validity of one year. You must renew the card every year, similar to the process before the introduction of the Single Permit.

Additional Information
Based on community experience, an Indian applicant in Brussels received approval for an Unlimited Work Permit after 3 years of residence in Belgium while living with family. The application was reportedly approved within one week. Individual processing times and eligibility may vary.

If your spouse, registered partner, or children are lawfully residing with you, you may qualify after three years of employment covered by a Single Permit or Work Permit, within ten years of lawful and uninterrupted residence in Belgium immediately preceding the application.

Please note: Brussels has specific eligibility conditions. The requirements in Flanders may differ. Always refer to the official regional authorities for the latest information.

items.question: Are you looking for a trash collection calendar in your area?
items.answer:
Yes. You can use the official Recycle! app to stay informed about waste collection schedules in your area.

The app allows you to:
- Receive collection reminders and notifications.
- Check the type of waste being collected on each collection day.
- View your local waste collection calendar based on your location.

You can find more information and download the app from:
https://recycleapp.be/

items.question: Where do I submit my tax return?
items.answer:
You may receive a brown envelope by post containing your Belgian income tax return. You can submit your tax return either:
- Online through the official Belgian tax portal, or
- By post/in person by submitting the completed paper form at the tax office.

Brussels Tax Office Address:
Boulevard du Jardin Botanique 50, 1000 Bruxelles (Inside Botanique Metro Station)

For more information, please refer to:
https://www.thebulletin.be/understanding-paper-belgian-income-tax-return
https://finance.belgium.be/en/about_fps/structure_and_services/general_administrations/taxation

Typical processing time is around 4 working days, although this may vary.

Alternatively, you can complete the Apostille process yourself at a minimal cost by following the official Government of India guidelines:

items.question: Are you looking to apply for a Professional Card to become a freelancer?
items.answer:
Yes. It is possible to apply for a Professional Card to work as a self-employed professional (freelancer) in Belgium.

Based on community experience, IT professionals generally require a client offer or contract that specifies their daily rate. Requirements for other professions may differ depending on the nature of the business and regional regulations.

Please note: The information below applies to applicants who do not yet hold Permanent Residence (PR). Individual circumstances and eligibility requirements may vary.

Estimated Costs:
- Professional Card application: €180
- VAT registration: Approximately €40
- Cross-bank account opening: Charges vary depending on the bank.
- Accountant consultation: Fees depend on your business plan and the services required.

Overall setup costs may range between €1,200 and €1,400, depending on your circumstances and the service providers you choose.

Companies that can assist with Professional Card applications:
https://www.partena-professional.be/en
https://www.acerta.be/nl
https://www.liantis.be/nl
https://www.securex.be/nl

Disclaimer: Requirements, fees, and processing times may change. Always verify the latest information with the official authorities or the service provider before submitting your application.

items.answer:

Note: Most households in Belgium have separate Day and Night electricity meter readings, depending on the type of installation.

Some of the popular energy providers are:

To compare suppliers and prices, you can use the following websites:

Energy Price Simulator:
https://www.energyprice.be/compare

items.question: How do I obtain a car driving licence in Belgium?
items.answer:
Obtaining a Belgian driving licence is a step-by-step process. The exact path depends on your previous driving experience.

Step 1: Pass the Theory Test
Before taking the theory test, ensure that you have a valid Belgian Residence Permit.

How to Prepare:
1. Study the Theory — Purchase an English-translated theory book or buy a second-hand copy through Facebook groups, or study online using browser translation tools. Theory lessons: https://rijbewijsb.gratisrijbewijsonline.be/theorie (click 'Les' to access the lessons). English learning cards: https://www.mondikaarten.be/driving-licence-b-full-package-1608
2. Practice Online — Practice with mock theory exams before booking your official test. Paid practice tests: https://www.drivinglicence-belgium.be/ Free practice tests: https://rijbewijs-online.be/en/belgium/practice-test (you can use Chrome's translation feature if required)
3. Attend the Theory Exam — Book and take the theory exam at an approved examination centre.
4. If You Do Not Pass — You may retake the theory exam. If you fail two consecutive attempts, you must complete compulsory theory training through an approved driving school before attempting the exam again.

Step 2: Practical Driving Test
The process differs depending on your driving experience.

Option A – If You Already Have Driving Experience
You may take practical lessons with a driving school and then appear for the practical driving test. Recommended: at least 6 hours of driving lessons before your practical exam.
Approximate costs: Driving School €650, Translator €65, Practical Exam €40.
If you do not pass, you may retake the practical exam as many times as required. Approximate cost per additional attempt: Driving School €250, Translator €65, Practical Exam €40.
Important: Avoid making serious mistakes during the practical test, as repeated major errors may require you to repeat the theory process.

Option B – If You Are a New Driver
If you have little or no driving experience, you must complete 20 hours of practical training with an approved driving school. After completing the training, you will receive a Provisional Driving Licence.
With a provisional licence: you may drive your own vehicle independently; in certain cases, an accompanying driver with at least 8 years of driving experience may supervise you (the accompanying driver must be registered with the commune and may be required to complete a 4-hour training session).
Approximate costs: 20-hour Driving Course €1,000, Provisional Licence approximately €20 (may vary by commune).
After 3 months, you can take the practical driving test using either your own vehicle or a driving school vehicle. Based on community experience, ASSE is often recommended as an examination centre.
Approximate practical test costs: Driving School €250, Translator €65, Practical Exam €40.

Additional Requirements in Brussels
If you are applying in the Brussels Region, you must also attend a First Aid training session, and pass the Perception Test separately if you do not speak one of the local languages (a translator may be required).

For detailed guidance, please refer to:
https://abroadhandbook.com/2020/01/29/how-do-you-get-driving-belgium-licence/

Practice Resources:
GOCA Sample Video: https://youtu.be/zKh0zAbHeJQ
Perception Test Practice App: http://onelink.to/perception

Disclaimer: The process, fees, and requirements may change over time and may vary by region. Always verify the latest information with the official authorities or your chosen driving school before applying.

Based on community experience, consultations typically last 1–2 hours, depending on the complexity of your case. The duration and fees may vary for each individual.

items.question: How can I renew my, my spouse's, or my child's Indian passport in Belgium or Luxembourg?
items.answer:

Please refer to the official Indian Embassy website for the latest passport renewal requirements and application process.

Chaussée de Waterloo 699, 1180 Uccle, Belgium (Near the Indian Embassy)

The above information is based on our community's experience with renewing Indian passports in Belgium and Luxembourg. Always verify the latest requirements with the Indian Embassy before submitting your application.

items.answer:

Important: The sender (you, your family members, friends, or neighbours) should have a valid medical prescription before shipping medicines.

Courier Service: http://fastwayindia.com/ (FastWayIndia)

Note: Based on our experience, we successfully shipped medicines from Hyderabad using this service without any issues. Delivery times and charges may vary depending on the shipment and destination.

Please contact the customer support team through their official website for the latest pricing and shipping requirements.

items.question: Are you looking for the process of bringing your spouse or dependent to Belgium?
items.answer:
If you are already living in Belgium and wish to bring your spouse or dependent(s), you can apply for a Visa D (Family Reunification) by following the applicable requirements.

Estimated Processing Time:
- If you apply within 6 months of your arrival in Belgium: Dependent visa approval may take approximately 1–2 weeks (based on community experience).
- If you apply after 6 months of your arrival: Processing may take 6–9 months, depending on your individual circumstances and the authorities' processing times.

Documents Required from the Sponsor (Applicant in Belgium):
- Copy of your Residence Permit (front and back).
- Registered rental agreement. Brussels Registration Office: Boulevard du Jardin Botanique 50, 1000 Bruxelles
- Copy of your Single Permit.
- Latest 6 months' salary slips (or eligible income proof, where applicable).
- Proof of payment of the administrative fee (can be paid using Bancontact).
- Copy of the identity page of your passport.

Documents Required from Your Spouse and/or Children:
- Valid passport (recommended validity of at least 12 months).
- Separate online Visa D application form for each applicant.
- Passport-size photographs meeting Belgian visa specifications.
- Birth Certificate (to be legalized where applicable).
- Medical Certificate issued by an authorized hospital.
- Police Clearance Certificate (to be legalized where applicable).
- Marriage Certificate (to be legalized where applicable).
- Travel insurance covering a minimum of €30,000 in medical expenses for at least 3 months.

Application Process:
- Once all documents are ready, book an appointment with your nearest VFS centre.
- VFS may offer Standard and Express appointment options depending on the location.
- Biometric fingerprints will be collected during your appointment.
- Ensure you carry the applicable visa fee for each applicant.

Disclaimer: The above information is based on community experience and may vary depending on individual circumstances and changes in immigration regulations. Please refer to the official Belgian authorities and VFS guidelines before submitting your application.

items.question: How long does it take for a dependent or child visa to be approved?
items.answer:
Based on community experience:
- If you arrived in Belgium less than 6 months ago: Visa approval may take approximately 1–2 weeks after submission, although public holidays and individual circumstances may affect processing times.
- If you arrived more than 6 months ago: Processing may take 3–6 months, with the official processing time being up to 9 months, depending on your employment status and the immigration authorities.

Applications should be submitted through the appropriate VFS centre in your country of residence.

Disclaimer: Processing times are based on community experiences and may vary from case to case.

items.question: Are you looking for weekend activities or sports facilities?
items.answer:
If you're looking for a healthy weekend activity, Belgium offers several public swimming pools and sports facilities.

Tip: Carry your Residence Permit (RP) Card, as many facilities offer discounted multi-entry passes. Also remember to bring your own swimming costume, as some pools charge additional fees for rentals or purchases.

Public Swimming Pools:
- Pool Laeken
- Pool Brussels
- Pool Neder-Over-Heembeek
- Pool Evere
- Pool Zaventem
- Pool Watermael-Boitsfort

Please check the respective pool websites for admission fees and opening hours.

Sports Halls:
If you enjoy badminton, basketball, football, or other indoor sports, you may consider the following venues:
- Pole Nord
- Wolu Sports Park
- Kortenberg
- Kraainem

If you know of additional sports facilities, please let us know so we can include them in this list.

items.question: Are you looking for day care or crèches in Brussels?
items.answer:
Brussels has many childcare centres (crèches) for young children.

Enrolling your child in a crèche provides opportunities for:
- Social interaction with other children.
- Learning through play.
- Early childhood development in a structured environment.

Childcare fees vary depending on your family situation, income, and employment status.

The information provided here is based on community knowledge. We recommend checking official local resources to find the most suitable childcare option for your family.

items.question: Are you looking to open a bank account online?
items.answer:
Opening a bank account in Belgium is simple and can often be completed online.

Before starting your application, ensure that you have a scanned copy of your Residence Permit Card (front and back).

Some of the popular banks include:
- ING
- Hello Bank (BNP Paribas Fortis)
- KBC
- Beobank (branch visit may be required)
- TransferWise (Wise)
- N26
- Other Belgian banks

Requirements and account features may vary between banks, so it is recommended to compare their services before opening an account.

items.question: Is there anything families and individuals should be cautious about while living in Brussels or Belgium?
items.answer:
Many of us have a tradition of wearing or carrying gold jewellery, which often holds significant sentimental and financial value. However, it is important to be aware of the risk of theft.

Based on community experiences, thefts involving gold jewellery and valuables have occurred in and around Brussels and other parts of Belgium.

Safety Tips:
- Avoid wearing or carrying large amounts of gold jewellery in public whenever possible.
- Do not keep valuable jewellery or large amounts of cash at home without proper security measures.
- Consider insuring your valuables for their current market value.
- Some banks, such as Beobank, offer safe deposit lockers (annual charges may apply) where you can securely store valuable items.
- If you own valuable jewellery, obtain a valuation certificate from a reputable jewellery store for insurance purposes.

Recommendation: If possible, avoid bringing large quantities of gold jewellery to Belgium. While it may represent years of savings and memories, recovering stolen valuables can be difficult.

items.question: Where can I buy fresh or live fish in Belgium?
items.answer:
If you are looking for fresh or live fish similar to what is available in your hometown, you may consider visiting the following places:
- Clemenceau Market
- Fish Shop
- CORA (Woluwe, Anderlecht)

Note: These locations are provided as general references based on community experience. You can also search for similar fish markets closer to your location.

items.question: Are there theatres where I can watch movies in Belgium?
items.answer:
Yes. Belgium has several popular cinema chains showing international, regional, and local movies.

Many theatres also offer:
- Group ticket discounts.
- Membership cards.
- Discount vouchers available through nearby kiosks or promotional offers.

Popular cinema chains include:
- UGC
- Kinepolis
- White Cinemas
- Cinema Galeries

We recommend checking each theatre's website for the latest movie schedules, pricing, and promotional offers.

items.question: Which mobile network should I choose in Belgium?

items.question: Where can I buy Belgian chocolates to take home?
items.answer:

Leonidas: Industrielaan 8, 1070 Anderlecht
